_comment: Sentry dashboard definitions for the Kassa pilot. Owned by KASA-294; applied via scripts/observability-apply.sh. Schema is the Sentry v0 organization-dashboards API (https://docs.sentry.io/api/dashboards/). The `title` field is the idempotency key — the apply script reconciles by title within `(organization, project_slug)`. Do not rename dashboards without also renaming the Sentry record. `project_slug` is a local routing key; the apply script resolves it against the org's project list and lifts the numeric project id into the dashboard envelope's `projects: []` array, then drops `project_slug` before sending. Placeholders in `${ALL_CAPS}` form are env-substituted by the apply script — see infra/observability/README.md for the variable list.

dashboards._comment: KASA-294: RUM Web Vitals dashboard. One widget per Core Web Vital (LCP, INP, CLS), grouped by the `web-vitals.rating` tag the harness in apps/pos/src/lib/web-vitals.ts ships (good/needs-improvement/poor). The current harness writes the numeric metric value to `extra.*`, which Discover cannot p75 — so the widgets show event-count by rating instead of percentile-of-value. The web-vitals package's `rating:poor` bucket is exactly the >Google-poor-threshold value (LCP >4 s, INP >500 ms, CLS >0.25), so the `poor` segment of these widgets is the queryable form of the PO's `p75 LCP > 4s over 7d` ask. A follow-up (KASA-295) tracks emitting a real measurement on a web-vitals transaction so a `percentile(measurements.lcp, 0.75)` widget can replace these counts without changing the runbook contract.
dashboards.title: POS Web Vitals
